methodsAttempted.query: site:uni-mannheim.de Universität Mannheim KI Leitlinie ChatGPT generative KI Prüfungen Studierende
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official University of Mannheim ChatGPT handouts, a faculty thesis FAQ, and unrelated research/programme PDFs.
Firecrawl search found the central KI-FAQs page, official ChatGPT handouts, and an official newsroom teaching story.
Firecrawl search found the central AI overview, the KI-Richtlinie page, and the ZLL AI-in-teaching support page.
robots.txt allows User-agent * for the fetched public HTML and PDF paths; no restricted intranet links were fetched.

artifacts.summary: Official University of Mannheim discovery found a central Rectorate-approved KI-Richtlinie, central KI-FAQs, a ZLL AI-in-teaching support page, and official ChatGPT handouts for students and teachers. A faculty thesis FAQ, newsroom article, and MADOC research paper were rejected for public tracker claims because they are respectively faculty-scoped, news/story content, or research-only. The run found a central binding AI directive and therefore does not rely on older handouts as university-wide policy.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official central University of Mannheim page states the Rectorate approved the AI directive on 2025-10-15 and includes binding sections on scope, high-risk AI, AI register, data handling, responsibility, and transparency.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-FAQs: Fragen & Antworten zur KI-Richtlinie | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official central FAQ page explains the AI directive's effective date, scope, teaching/study use, high-risk AI, AI register, and data-handling rules.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Künstliche Intelligenz in der Lehre | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official Zentrum für Lehren und Lernen page provides AI-in-teaching orientation, workshops, courses, and consultation support; used only for support/training claims.

Do not generalize faculty, chair, newsroom, or research repository results into university-wide policy claims.

artifacts.claimText: Universität Mannheim has a Rectorate-approved AI directive that sets binding rules for members and affiliates on developing, procuring, using, controlling, placing on the market, and putting AI systems into operation.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Das Rektorat der Universität Mannheim hat in seiner Sitzung vom 15. Oktober 2025 die nachstehende KI-Richtlinie beschlossen. Diese Richtlinie legt für Mitglieder und Angehörige an der Universität Mannheim ... verbindliche Regeln ... fest.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Rectorate approved the AI directive on 15 October 2025; it sets binding rules for members and affiliates of Universität Mannheim.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Introductory paragraph and § 1 Zweck und Anwendungsbereich
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a central-source status and binding-rules claim because the official page expressly says the Rectorate approved the directive and § 1 states binding rules.

artifacts.claimText: In the service context, Universität Mannheim's directive allows members and affiliates to develop, procure, use, operate, place on the market, or put high-risk AI systems into operation only after the Rectorate has been informed and has expressly consented.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Hochrisiko-KI-Systeme dürfen im dienstlichen Kontext durch Mitglieder und Angehörige der Universität Mannheim nur entwickelt, beschafft, verwendet, in Betrieb genommen oder in Verkehr gebracht werden, soweit das Rektorat vorab hierüber informiert worden ist und dem ausdrücklich zugestimmt hat.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: High-risk AI systems in the service context require prior Rectorate information and express consent.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because the official directive uses mandatory language for high-risk AI systems in the service context.

artifacts.claimText: For service-context use, Universität Mannheim requires AI systems to be entered in the central AI register before development, procurement, operation, or market launch; confidential or personal data may be processed only in systems explicitly released for that purpose in the AI register, and systems marked not approved may not be used.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Im dienstlichen Kontext sind KI-Systeme vor ihrer Entwicklung, Beschaffung, Inbetriebnahme sowie vor ihrem Inverkehrbringen ... in das zentrale KI-Register einzutragen ... entsprechende Daten ausschließlich in KI-Systemen, welche im KI-Register ausdrücklich als hierfür freigegeben markiert sind.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Service-context AI systems must be entered in the central AI register; confidential or personal data may be processed only in systems released for that purpose.

artifacts.notes: Approved with service-context scope and data-handling limitation directly from §§ 5-6.

artifacts.claimText: Universität Mannheim assigns users responsibility for the correctness, legal permissibility, and dissemination of AI-generated content, says AI outputs should be checked and critically assessed before further use, and requires transparent disclosure where a notice obligation applies.

citation.citationTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-Richtlinie: Leitlinien für den Einsatz von KI | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Die Verantwortung für die Richtigkeit, die rechtliche Zulässigkeit der Nutzung sowie die Verbreitung von Inhalten, welche mit Hilfe von KI-Systemen erstellt wurden, tragen die Nutzerinnen und Nutzer ... KI-generierte Aussagen, Literatur, Quellen und Ergebnisse sind daher immer zu überprüfen.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Users are responsible for AI-generated content and should always check AI-generated statements, literature, sources, and results before further use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved because § 6 directly states user responsibility, checking obligations, human final responsibility, and transparency obligations where notice duties apply.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Mannheim AI FAQ says AI systems may be used in teaching when the system is university-approved, or the use is voluntary with no disadvantage for non-use, or the use is in elective courses with sufficient non-AI alternatives; it expressly forbids contexts where students are disadvantaged for non-use without an equivalent alternative.

citation.citationTitle: KI-FAQs: Fragen & Antworten zur KI-Richtlinie | Universität Mannheim
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: KI-FAQs: Fragen & Antworten zur KI-Richtlinie | Universität Mannheim
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: dürfen Sie KI-Systeme in der Lehre einsetzen, wenn 1) die Nutzung von der Universität freigegeben wurde oder 2) die Nutzung freiwillig ist und den Studierenden bei Nicht-Nutzung kein Nachteil für ihr Studium entsteht oder wenn 3) die Nutzung in Wahlveranstaltungen vorgesehen ist.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AI systems may be used in teaching if university-approved, voluntary without student disadvantage, or used in elective offerings with alternatives.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: FAQ section on AI use in teaching

artifacts.claimText: The University of Mannheim AI FAQ says AI use in study is possible depending on context, but for each exam students should clarify with the responsible teacher whether and under what conditions AI-system use is permitted; serious non-compliance may lead to final failure of an exam component.

artifacts.claimText: In an official 2023 teacher handout, Universität Mannheim's Zentrum für Lehren und Lernen advised teachers that ChatGPT is neither generally allowed nor generally prohibited and that teachers should decide for each exam whether and under what conditions students may use it and communicate that decision in time.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: ChatGPT in der Lehre
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Die Nutzung von ChatGPT ist weder grundsätzlich erlaubt noch grundsätzlich verboten! Sie müssen für jede Ihrer Prüfungen festlegen, ob und unter welchen Bedingungen die Studierenden ChatGPT nutzen dürfen, also ob KI ein zugelassenes Hilfsmittel ist. Diese Entscheidung müssen Sie rechtzeitig bekanntgeben.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Teachers are advised to set exam-specific ChatGPT conditions and communicate them in time.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as historical teacher guidance from an official ZLL handout, with the newer 2025 AI directive treated as the stronger central policy source.

artifacts.title: Universität Mannheim AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official University of Mannheim sources show a central AI directive approved by the Rectorate on 15 October 2025 and effective according to the FAQ on 4 November 2025. Claims in this bundle are scoped to that central directive, the official FAQ, the ZLL AI-in-teaching page, and two official ChatGPT handouts. The run rejected a faculty master-thesis FAQ, an editorial/news teaching story, and a research repository paper as insufficient for university-wide policy claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The central AI register and some training modules are linked through intranet/CAS/ILIAS paths and were not fetched because they require authenticated access.
Older 2023 ChatGPT PDF handouts were retained only as official guidance snapshots and not treated as superseding the 2025 AI directive.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
